title: Regular Expressions
content:
### Regular Expressions

A **regular expression (RegEx)** is a pattern used to **match** character combinations in strings.

---

### Creating a RegEx

```js
// Literal syntax (preferred)
const pattern = /hello/;

// Constructor
const pattern = new RegExp("hello");
```

---

### RegEx Methods

#### `test()` -- Returns Boolean

```js
const pattern = /world/;
console.log(pattern.test("Hello world!")); // true
console.log(pattern.test("Hi there!"));    // false
```

#### `exec()` -- Returns Match Details

```js
const result = /\d+/.exec("Age: 25");
console.log(result[0]); // "25"
console.log(result.index); // 5
```

---

### String Methods with RegEx

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `str.match(pattern)` | Returns matches | `"a1b2".match(/\d/g)` |
| `str.search(pattern)` | Returns index of first match | `"abc".search(/b/)` |
| `str.replace(pattern, new)` | Replace matches | `"a-b-c".replace(/-/g, ":")` |
| `str.split(pattern)` | Split by pattern | `"a,b,c".split(/,/)` |

---

### Common Patterns

| Pattern | Matches | Example |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `\d` | Any digit | `"5"` |
| `\w` | Any word char (a-z, A-Z, 0-9, _) | `"a"` |
| `\s` | Any whitespace | space, tab, newline |
| `.` | Any character (except newline) | `"x"` |
| `^` | Start of string | `/^Hello/` |
| `$` | End of string | `/world$/` |
| `*` | 0 or more | `/a*/` |
| `+` | 1 or more | `/a+/` |
| `?` | 0 or 1 (optional) | `/colou?r/` |
| `{n}` | Exactly n | `/\d{3}/` |
| `[abc]` | Any of a, b, or c | `/[aeiou]/` |
| `[a-z]` | Range a to z | `/[a-z]/` |

---

### Flags

| Flag | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `g` | Global (find all matches) |
| `i` | Case-insensitive |
| `m` | Multi-line (^ and $ match line boundaries) |

```js
/hello/gi;  // Global, case-insensitive
```

---

> **Use Cases:** Email validation, password strength, search/replace, data extraction, URL parsing.

quiz.question: Which flag makes a RegEx search global (find all matches)?
